app.name: DeepL
app.bestFor: Most natural output for European languages and professional documents, with glossaries and format fidelity.

app.name: Google Translate
app.bestFor: Broadest language coverage (240+ languages) and a free tier; best when you need reach over polish.

app.name: ChatGPT
app.bestFor: Tone-sensitive and creative translation where context and idiom matter more than raw language coverage.

app.name: Claude
app.bestFor: Strong on idioms, context, and Asian languages; a top pick for nuance-heavy passages.

app.name: Microsoft Translator
app.bestFor: Business communication and Office-integrated workflows with reliable API scale.

app.name: Lara Translate
app.bestFor: Context-aware MT (from the ModernMT team) marketed as a professional DeepL alternative with adaptive quality.

app.name: Amazon Translate
app.pricing: Paid (usage-based; free tier for first 12 months)
app.bestFor: High-volume, programmatic translation at scale inside AWS pipelines.

skill.name: openai-translator (yetone)
skill.bestFor: Wildly popular LLM-based translation and polishing tool (browser extension, desktop app, userscript); a recognized OSS translation leader (~25k stars).

skill.name: Immersive Translate
skill.bestFor: Bilingual side-by-side web-page, PDF, and ebook translation, loved for reading foreign content in context (~18k stars).

skill.name: LibreTranslate
skill.bestFor: The de-facto free, self-hosted machine-translation API — offline-capable, with no proprietary dependencies (~16k stars).

skill.name: translate-shell
skill.bestFor: Command-line translator wrapping Google/Bing/DeepL and more; a Unix-workflow staple for quick terminal translation (~7.5k stars).

skill.name: OpenNMT-py
skill.bestFor: Research-grade neural machine-translation toolkit for training and serving your own translation models (~7k stars).

skill.name: Argos Translate
skill.bestFor: Offline neural translation library (the engine under LibreTranslate) usable as a Python lib, CLI, or GUI (~6k stars).

skill.name: translate skill (feiskyer/claude-code-settings)
skill.bestFor: Well-maintained translate SKILL.md inside a popular curated Claude Code settings/skills collection (~1.6k stars overall).

skill.name: translate-book (deusyu)
skill.bestFor: Agent skill (Codex/Claude Code) that translates whole books — PDF/DOCX/EPUB — into any language using parallel subagents (~1k stars).

skill.name: translate skill (djyde/ccmate)
skill.bestFor: Localization-focused translate skill (updates existing locales, protects reserved terms); rated Grade A in the skills directory (~630 stars).

notes: There is no single winner in the app track — sources agree the right tool depends on workflow: DeepL for European/professional docs, Google for coverage, ChatGPT/Claude for tone and Asian languages. The Skill/Repo track is now ranked by real quality, reputation, and adoption (GitHub stars) rather than form factor — kind ('skill' vs 'repo') is only a tag. It mixes notable open-source translation repos (openai-translator, Immersive Translate, LibreTranslate, translate-shell, OpenNMT, Argos Translate) with the genuine translate Agent Skills that lead this still-emerging niche. The previous top-of-list translate-book skill now ranks below the far more adopted repos. Star counts verified via the GitHub API (Jul 2026); the tiny senshinji/claude-translation-skill (~14 stars) was dropped as below the quality bar.
